description: ByteDance's overseas news and video app, the English-language Toutiao. An early pre-TikTok bet, it shut in 2020 as users drained away.
ByteDance's video app, launched in Japan in 2016 and renamed from TopBuzz Video in 2018. TikTok buried it; ByteDance closed it in 2022.
ByteDance's overseas app for 15-second clips, built from its Hypstar and Flipagram buys. ByteDance closed it in 2020 and moved users to TikTok.
A US app for making music videos from photos and clips, an early TikTok forerunner. ByteDance's Toutiao bought it in 2017 and folded it into Vigo Video.
The 15-second lip-sync app that defined teen short video before TikTok. ByteDance bought it in 2017 and merged its ~100M users into TikTok in 2018.
Musical.ly's live-streaming app for broadcasting video and earning gifts. ByteDance closed it in 2018 and folded the streaming into Musical.ly.
A French-built global news app drawing on thousands of publishers. Cheetah Mobile then ByteDance owned it; ByteDance quietly closed it in 2021.
Indonesia's top news app, pulling stories from hundreds of outlets. ByteDance, its owner from 2018, closed it in 2022 and moved users to Helo.
ByteDance's social music app for emerging markets, built around scrolling lyrics. Folded into TikTok Music abroad; India pulled it in 2024 after a data probe.
ByteDance's paid music service and Resso's successor, tied to TikTok. ByteDance closed it after 16 months to steer listeners to Spotify and Apple Music.
ByteDance's social app for India, sharing memes and Bollywood clips in 14-plus languages. India banned it in 2020; ByteDance killed it in 2021 when it quit India.
ByteDance's light WhatsApp rival for young Africans. After three years and ~7M downloads it never escaped WhatsApp; ByteDance closed it in 2024.
TikTok's photo-and-text app, a grid-feed Instagram rival. Tested only in Canada, Australia and Vietnam, it closed in 2025; ByteDance pushed users to Lemon8.
ByteDance's US book imprint, built to sell #BookTok romance and YA. After ~2 years and 30-odd titles it barely used its TikTok reach and closed in 2025.
ByteDance's camera app turning selfies into 3D avatars. Launched in Southeast Asia in 2021, it drew little interest; ByteDance dropped it by early 2022.
A watch-to-earn feature in TikTok Lite paying users points to watch and invite friends. EU regulators flagged addiction risks; ByteDance pulled it in 2024.
ByteDance's flagship global short-video app, launched in 2017 as China's Douyin abroad. Still alive; never shut down, though banned in India since 2020.
ByteDance's free short-video editor, born from China's Jianying. Still alive and among the world's most-used editors; only India has banned it outright.
ByteDance's Xiaohongshu-style lifestyle app for beauty, food and travel. US stores pulled it in 2025 under PAFACA, then restored it; still live abroad.
ByteDance's free AI photo editor, the overseas twin of China's Xingtu. US stores pulled it under PAFACA in 2025 and never restored it; it lives on elsewhere.
ByteDance's AI homework-help app, launched in 2020 as a photo math solver and renamed Gauth in 2024. US stores pulled it under PAFACA in 2025, then restored it.
ByteDance's work suite for chat, video, docs and calendar, China's Feishu abroad. US stores pulled it in 2025 under PAFACA, but it stays alive worldwide.
A hit free-to-play card game by US studio Second Dinner, published worldwide by ByteDance's Nuverse. It lives on, but dropped ByteDance for Skystone Games in 2025.
A free-to-play 5v5 mobile MOBA, huge in Southeast Asia and Latin America. Not shut down: ByteDance agreed in 2026 to sell maker Moonton to Saudi Arabia for over $6B.
ByteDance's close-friends photo app, like BeReal, run under TikTok in ~71 countries (not the US, UK or Europe) and later steered toward chat. Still live.
ByteDance's Quora/Zhihu-style Q&A app under Toutiao. Despite ~RMB 1bn in subsidies it never dented Zhihu; ByteDance closed it in 2021 and folded it into Toutiao.
ByteDance's paid-knowledge app for adult self-improvement, a Dedao rival. ByteDance closed it in 2021 after weak sales and no breakout courses.
ByteDance's Snapchat-style messenger, one of three 2019 apps meant to topple WeChat. Tencent blocked it on day one; ByteDance folded it into Douyin in 2021.
ByteDance's messenger-plus-groups app, its second try against WeChat. Apple pulled it within two months; it never caught on, and ByteDance killed it in 2021.
ByteDance's Instagram-style photo community, launched days after Duoshan in 2019. Starved of support, it never caught on; ByteDance pulled it within six months.
Toutiao's Xiaohongshu-style lifestyle app where users found products but left to buy. ByteDance closed it after a year, having never dented Xiaohongshu.
ByteDance's discount shopping app inside Toutiao, built to answer Pinduoduo. Lacking WeChat Pay and group-buying, it flopped; ByteDance pulled it within a year.
ByteDance's very first app (2012, before Toutiao), a hugely popular Chinese joke and meme community. China's regulator shut it for good in 2018 over vulgar content.
ByteDance's humor and witty-comment meme community, launched in 2018 to absorb users from the banned Neihan Duanzi. Still active and well-kept in 2026.
ByteDance's first metaverse-style app, a 3D world where avatars hung out and joined virtual concerts. ByteDance pulled it in 2022 and shut it after cutting the team.
A ByteDance reading app that dropped its algorithm for human-curated interest circles. Invite-only and slow to grow, it closed eight months after launch.
A Chinese fan community for cosplayers, illustrators and fan-fiction writers, launched in 2014. ByteDance bought it in 2018 and shut it in 2023.
ByteDance's women-focused beauty community, built by its acquired studio Bcy. Its first stab at copying Xiaohongshu, it flopped and was pulled within a year.
A Chinese beauty-selfie camera with live smoothing, face-swaps and filters. ByteDance bought it in 2019, then took it offline as one camera too many.
A fashion shopping app from Douyin's e-commerce arm, a Dewu/Xiaohongshu rival. ByteDance wound it down after ~9 months once Douyin's own shopping made it redundant.
ByteDance's app pairing Chinese children with live North American English teachers, a VIPKid rival. China's 2021 "double reduction" crackdown killed it.
ByteDance's cartoon-dragon tutoring app for kids 2-8. China's 2021 "double reduction" crackdown gutted it; ByteDance later shut it for good.
ByteDance's K-12 live-tutoring app, built from its 2019 buy of Hua Luogeng Online School. China's 2021 "double reduction" ban gutted Dali Education and closed it.
ByteDance's online-learning app, launched in 2020 and later rebuilt as a Douyin creator-course tool. The standalone app closed in 2024, absorbed into Douyin Classroom.
ByteDance's English app pairing a Douyin-style video feed with AI pronunciation scoring. Launched in 2019; China's 2021 "double reduction" crackdown closed it.
An animated math app teaching logic to kids 3-12 in small live classes. ByteDance bought it in 2020 and shut it in 2021 when "double reduction" outlawed it.
ByteDance's K-12 live-tutoring app, built on the acquired Qingbei Online School. The Dali Ketang name lasted two weeks before reverting to Qingbei Wangxiao.
ByteDance's photo-homework app for Indian students in grades 6-12. ByteDance closed it in 2021 when it quit India's edtech market.

original_name: 大力智能学习灯 (Dali Smart Learning Lamp)
description: ByteDance's first hardware: a kids' smart desk lamp with a touchscreen and cameras for AI homework help. China's 2021 "double reduction" crackdown sank the line by 2022.
ByteDance's ad-free search app, a Baidu challenger. It never dented Baidu and, in the post-ChatGPT rush, was renamed Xiao Wukong in 2023 and made an AI assistant.
A short-lived ByteDance search app built on Toutiao's content. It lasted barely three months before stalling at its 2023 update and vanishing from China's app stores.
ByteDance's web search app, built to challenge Baidu. It never displaced the leader and in 2023 became Youshi, a lifestyle community; web search lives on in Toutiao.
